# llms.txt — Craft Kit

Project: Craft Kit
URL: https://craft-kit.dev
Repository: https://github.com/handplant/craftcms-lazy-starter-kit
Author: Andi Grether — https://webworker.me
License: MIT

Description:
Craft Kit is a production-ready Craft CMS 5 starter kit for agencies.
It demonstrates a hypermedia-first architecture using Craft CMS, Twig,
and Datastar to build reactive websites without SPA frameworks.

Focus:
- Server-driven rendering and state
- Hypermedia (HTML over the wire)
- Minimal JavaScript, no hydration
- High performance and long-term maintainability
- Agency-friendly Craft CMS architectures

Key Concepts:
- Hypermedia-driven UI updates
- Datastar signals and DOM morphing
- Progressive enhancement
- Clear separation of content, layout, and interaction

Technology Stack:
- Craft CMS 5
- Twig
- Datastar
- Tailwind CSS v4
- Vite
- DDEV
- Vue 3 Web Components (for isolated UI elements)
- PHP

Included Examples:
- Live search: https://craft-kit.dev/search
- Filtered and paginated blog: https://craft-kit.dev/blog-datastar
- API-backed views (Rick & Morty): https://craft-kit.dev/rick-and-morty-datastar
- Interactive maps: https://craft-kit.dev/map
- Matrix-based content builder: https://craft-kit.dev/matrix-builder

Blog:
- Datastar vs. Alpine + HTMX — Why Datastar Is the Better Fit for Craft CMS:
  https://craft-kit.dev/blog/datastar-vs-alpine-htmx-why-datastar-is-the-better-fit-for-craft-cms

Intended Audience:
- Digital agencies
- Craft CMS developers
- Long-lived client projects
- LLM-based tooling analyzing modern server-first architectures

Expert Contact:
Andi Grether is a freelance Craft CMS developer specializing in
hypermedia-first architectures and the Datastar stack.
https://webworker.me
